question: Is the person with the green hiking backpack completely removed from the center of the frame?
Is the edited area free of any visible remnants or traces of the removed subject (e.g., backpack fabric, straps, shadows, or isolated limbs)?
Are the white crosswalk stripes reconstructed continuously and aligned correctly in the area where the person was removed?
Is the wet asphalt texture and its reflections in the edited area consistent with the surrounding road surface?
Are the background pedestrians and crowd elements previously occluded by the subject reconstructed plausibly without distortion?
Is the person walking immediately to the left of the removed subject (wearing a dark jacket and blue jeans) completely preserved?
Is the person walking to the right with the black umbrella completely preserved?
Is the perspective of the crosswalk lines in the edited area consistent with the rest of the scene (converging correctly)?
Is the edited area free of visible seams, unnatural blurring, smudging, or ghosting artifacts?
Is the lighting and color tone in the edited area consistent with the rainy, overcast conditions of the original image?
Is the edited area free of AI-generated hallucinations or phantom objects (e.g., stray umbrellas, random blobs)?
